en: Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP) - CalOSHA
Avetta PQF - Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP) - CalOSHA
Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP)

Heat Illness Prevention: This standard applies to all outdoor places of employment. In addition, the following industries are subject to additional requirements in high heat (temperature that equals or exceeds 95 F): 1. Agriculture, 2. Construction, 3. Landscaping, 4. Oil and gas extraction, and 5. Transportation and delivery of agricultural products and of construction or other heavy materials (e.g., furniture, lumber, freight, cargo, cabinets, industrial or commercial materials). https://www.dir.ca.gov/dosh/heatillnessinfo.html
Heat Illness Prevention (T8 CCR Section 3395)

Written IIPP: Please upload a copy of your current IIPP for review and verification. A written, effective Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP) is required for every California employer.\n\n https://www.dir.ca.gov/dosh/etools/09-031/index.htm
Please upload a copy of your written Injury and Illness Prevention Program (IIPP). NOTE: The IIPP requires an annual review to ensure effectiveness.

Annual Review: Please ensure the IIPP addresses the maintenance, review and evaluation, at least annually, to ensure effectiveness of the program.\n\n https://www.dir.ca.gov/dosh/etools/09-031/index.htm

Compliance: Please ensure the IIPP addresses a system for ensuring employees will comply with safe and healthy work practices.\n https://www.dir.ca.gov/dosh/etools/09-031/index.htm
Does the written IIPP include a system to ensure employees comply with safe and health work practices?

Communication: Please ensure the IIPP states how safety and health will be communicated in a format that is readily understandable by all employees. This may include formats such as safety meetings documented training, postings, anonymous means used to notify the employer of unsafe work conditions or practices, safety committees or other written communications used to communicate safety issues with employees.\n https://www.dir.ca.gov/dosh/etools/09-031/index.htm \n

Training and Instruction: Please sure the IIPP addresses initial/ongoing training and instruction for employees. An effective Injury and Illness Prevention Program includes training for both supervisors and employees. Training for both is required by Cal/OSHA safety orders. https://www.dir.ca.gov/dosh/etools/09-031/index.htm

Heat Illness Prevention Program: The employer must develop, put in writing, and implement effective procedures for complying with the requirements of T8 CCR 3395. The Heat Illness Prevention Plan includes the following: • Procedures for providing sufficient water • Procedures for providing access to shade • High-heat procedures • Emergency response procedures • Acclimatization methods and procedures The Heat Illness Prevention Plan must be written both in English and in the language understood by the majority of employees. It must be available to employees at the worksite, as well as to representatives of Cal/OSHA upon request. It may be integrated into the employer's Injury and Illness Prevention Program. https://www.dir.ca.gov/dosh/heatillnessinfo.html
Has a Heat Illness Prevention Plan compliant with the requirements of T8 Section 3395 be developed for work being performed by employees exposed to high temperatures?
